---
title: "Project Charta"
subtitle: "Exploring the Aircraft Design Space:<br>An Interactive Visualization of 27’714 Aircraft Configurations"
author:
  - "Phineas Berdelis"
  - "Zina Aziz"
format: html
---

---

# Context and Scope

This project is situated in the domain of data science and aerospace engineering, focusing on the exploration of a large-scale aircraft design data set (“AircraftVerse”) which contains 27’714 simulated aircraft configurations.

Each configuration includes

- Design parameters: e.g. wingspan, rotor count, battery size, wing shape  
- Performance metrics: e.g. flight range, hover time, energy efficiency  

## Key Questions

- Which simulated aircraft designs are viable at all?
- Which design families perform best for range, hover time and speed?
- Which recurring design recipes show strong trade-offs between performance and mass?

## Expected Benefits

- Users get a clear overview of all aircraft designs and can distinguish viable from non-viable designs.
- Users understand which combinations of design parameters lead to better performance.
- Users identify meaningful patterns in the design space that are not visible from individual aircraft designs alone.

## Publication Platform

The visualization will be published as an interactive web application accessible to students and instructors.

---

# Project Objectives and Success Criteria

## Objectives

- Support comparison of different aircraft design families.
- Enable users to identify high-performing aircraft configurations using multiple criteria.
- Help users explore and understand how design parameters affect performance metrics.

## Success Criteria

**Qualitative**

- Users can clearly identify at least three meaningful relationships between design and performance  
- The visualization is easy to use  

**Quantitative**

- The users will be completing tasks (for example finding the best design) in less than two minutes  
- The dashboard maintains a smooth performance with 27,714 data points  

## Out of Scope

- The project only uses simulated data. Physical hardware will not be constructed  
- No advanced predictive modelling (for example machine learning)  
- There will be no detailed engineering validation or evaluation  

---

# Stakeholder Analysis

- Aviation students: Use the tool to understand aircraft design trade-offs in a school subject.
- Aviation lecturers / teaching assistants: Use the tool to support explanation and assessment.
- Data visualization instructors: Evaluate the clarity, reproducibility and appropriateness of the visualization product.

---

# User Analysis

## Persona 1: Aviation Student

- Domain expertise: basic aviation and aircraft technology knowledge, limited data visualization experience
- Technical environment: laptop during class, project work or exam preparation

**User tasks**

- Compare aircraft layouts and understand how design choices influence range, hover time and speed.
- Identify which aircraft families are more likely to be viable.
- Use visual evidence for a school task about aircraft design trade-offs.

**Pains**

- Technical generator names are difficult to interpret.
- A large simulated dataset can be overwhelming without grouping and filtering.
- Trade-offs between range, hover time, speed and mass are hard to compare in raw tables.

**Gains**

- Clear visual explanations of important aircraft design patterns.
- Ability to compare design families without reading raw JSON files.
- Support for subject-specific reasoning about viable aircraft configurations.

---

## Persona 2: Aviation Lecturer / Teaching Assistant

- Domain expertise: aviation and aircraft design fundamentals
- Technical environment: laptop or classroom projector

**User tasks**

- Use the dashboard to explain design-performance relationships.
- Check whether students can interpret simulated aircraft performance data.
- Provide examples of design trade-offs in class.

**Pains**

- Raw simulation data is too detailed for quick classroom use.
- Students may focus on individual outliers instead of robust patterns.
- Technical variable names need translation into understandable concepts.

**Gains**

- A focused tool for explaining aircraft configuration trade-offs.
- Group-level summaries that support teaching rather than isolated anecdotal examples.
- A reproducible visualization product that can be discussed and critiqued.

---

# Situation Assessment

## Resources

- Dataset: AircraftVerse  
- Tools: Python, Streamlit, Plotly, Pandas  
- Team: 2 Students  

## Constraints

- Limited time (semester project)  
- Data complexity (high-dimensional)  

## Risks

- Complex visualizations  
- Technical challenges such as performance  

---

# Visualization Concept

**Product Form:**  
An interactive dashboard combined with a data story  

## Visual Encodings

- Scatter plots for relationships (e.g. wingspan vs. range)  
- Histograms for distributions  
- Heatmaps for correlation overview  

## Interactivity

- Filtering  
- Hover tooltips: detailed info per design  
- Zoom in on design  

---

# Project Plan

```{mermaid}
gantt
    title Project Timeline
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD

    section Phase 1
    Data Understanding           :2026-03-19, 7d

    section Phase 2
    Concept Design               :2026-03-27, 10d

    section Phase 3
    Implementation               :2026-04-06, 35d

    section Phase 4
    Testing and Refinement       :2026-05-11, 14d

    section Phase 5
    Finalization & Presentation  :2026-05-25, 7d
```

## Phase 1: Data Understanding 

- Explore dataset  
- Define key variables  

## Phase 2: Concept Design

- Sketch visualizations  
- Define interactions  

## Phase 3: Implementation

- Build dashboard  
- Integrate user interactivity  

## Phase 4: Testing and Refinement

- User testing  
- Improve usability  

## Phase 5: Finalization and Presentation

- Final polish  
- Prepare presentation  

---

# GitHub Pages

Link zur Dokumentation: see the deployed GitHub Pages site for this repository.
